summary: The bridge packet correctly enforces opaque handles and advisory-only semantics, but requires follow-up to ensure finding keys remain stable across runs.

findings.title: Finding key stability verification needed
findings.summary: Evidence C suggests stable finding keys are ready, but the caveat notes that not all findings are equally portable; verification is needed to ensure keys remain generic across runs.

findings.title: Boundary caveat documentation gap
findings.summary: Evidence A identifies a missing explicit caveat boundary for reused adapter findings; documentation should be updated to clarify this constraint.

findings.title: Authority note clarity improvement
findings.summary: Evidence B indicates a need for clearer explanation of caller-owned authority post-handoff; the authority note should be tightened for public-safe templates.

decision.summary: The bridge packet correctly enforces opaque handles and advisory-only semantics, but requires follow-up to ensure finding keys remain stable across runs.

non_blocking_findings.title: Authority note clarity improvement

follow_up: Verify that all reusable finding keys remain generic and do not encode private naming or caller-owned identifiers.
Update documentation to include explicit caveat boundaries for reused adapter findings.
Refine the authority note to clearly state that downstream routing and writes remain caller-owned.
